---
title: "The Evolution of Computing"
format: pptx
---

## The Evolution of Computing: From Early Mainframes to the AI Era


## Introduction

- Overview of the computing journey
- Progression through levels of abstraction
- The impact of miniaturization
- The shift towards global connectivity

## The Era of Foundational Machines (1930s – 1960s)

- Use of vacuum tubes and mechanical relays
- The development of ENIAC
- Introduction of Von Neumann architecture
- Early mainframe computers

## The Personal Computing Revolution (1970s – 1980s)

- The advent of the microprocessor
- Early hobbyist machines: Altair 8800
- The rise of the Apple II
- IBM PC and the standardization of personal computing
- Introduction of Graphical User Interfaces (GUI) with Macintosh

## The Internet and the World Wide Web (1990s – early 2000s)

- Invention of the WWW by Tim Berners-Lee
- Emergence of HTTP and HTML protocols
- The impact of the Mosaic web browser
- Evolution from static web to Web 2.0 (interactivity)

## The Era of Mobile, Cloud, and AI (2010s – Present)

- The smartphone revolution led by the iPhone
- Adoption of Cloud Computing (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
- Ubiquity of mobile connectivity
- The rise of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)

## Summary Timeline

- 1930s-1960s: Foundational Machines (Mainframes, Vacuum Tubes)
- 1970s-1980s: Personal Computing (Microprocessors, GUI)
- 1990s-2000s: Internet Era (WWW, Web 2.0)
- 2010s-Present: Mobile, Cloud, & AI (Smartphones, LLMs)

## Conclusion

- Reflection on the rapid pace of technological change
- Looking ahead: Quantum computing and beyond
- The ongoing impact of computing on human civilization
